title: Forensics 101 🔍
description: How to preserve evidence without 'contaminating' the scene.

slides.title: Digital Evidence 🔍
slides.content:
### The Scene of the Crime

**Digital Forensics** is the science of finding and preserving evidence on computers. 

*   **Rule #1**: Never work on the original drive. Use a write-blocker and make a **Bit-stream Copy** (an exact clone) to work on.

slides.title: Order of Volatility ⌛
slides.content:
### Flash it or Cache it

Data disappears at different speeds. You must collect the 'fast' data first:

1.  **RAM (Memory)**: Disappears when you turn off the power. (Capture this first!)
2.  **Hard Drive**: Stays after power off. (Capture this last).

quizData.question: An investigator arrives at a hacked computer. What should they capture first?
quizData.explanation: RAM is highly volatile and will be lost if the computer is shut down or the power is pulled.
